real page now.
Yeah, I think so too.
Hi Frank.
Hello, how are you?
I am great.
How are you
doing?
Well, doing, doing well.
Oh, let me uh let me see.
I get my camera on here
now.
Frank, where are you located?
Are you in Connecticut?
I am in Connecticut.
Yeah.
Who?
Nice.
What part?
Um We Greg and I are both in Stanford.
Ok.
So I'm originally, I'm from Connecticut.
I say that very loosely.
I was born there.
I live in New Hampshire now, so I'm not too far from you guys.
Ok.
Got it.
Yeah, neither of us are actually from Connecticut.
We're uh we're both from Jersey.
Yeah.
Very nice.
Well, we appreciate you guys hopping on today.
Um I know that you're both busy.
Um And I also understand that you're fairly familiar with apartment IQ.
Is that correct?
Um I am uh Greg.
Yeah, Greg's not.
And when I say I'm familiar with it, I probably did a demo and signed a contract.
Um It's gotta be,
yeah, it's gotta be three years ago and we never actually used the product by the way, it never got integrated or set up and then we moved to real page.
So it just got lost.
OK?
So a lot has changed since then, I saw that, that you had signed on um a few years back and never went anywhere.
But you had an overview at the time since then, quite a bit has changed and we'll get into all of that.
Um But essentially the purpose of today's call is just to learn how you guys are utilizing competitive data, how you're gathering all of that information currently.
Um So we'll just understand what your current process is, any gaps and challenges you're seeing and then we'll give you a high level overview of apartment IQ if that works.
Ok.
So just curious as to um just how you guys are currently gathering comp data, market surveys and all that kind of stuff.
Yeah.
So right now it's pretty old school pen and paper way.

I mean, you know, I think they, the managers put it into a spreadsheet but um it's pretty much all at the site level, you know, they're either calling comps checking online, you know, maybe doing the occasional mystery shop or something, uh or secret shop, I should say not mystery shop.
Um And, and, and filling out the info in like a spreadsheet and it gets very subjective.
Um There's not a lot of thought that goes into different amenities and offerings at the communities or the unit types themselves.
You know, like unit level amenities less, maybe site amenities.
Like sometimes we can't really change the site amenities as much as we would like, but we could upgrade appliances or floors or things like that, like within the unit itself.
So it's very much at the site level.
And so again, it, it becomes very subjective when someone thinks like, oh, this is our direct comp but in reality, it's like different vintage, different offering, right?
Like different location, like just a lot of different things.
Um So that's our process right now and it's, it's just, it's very old school.
It's, I don't wanna say it's a mess, but it's just like, not consistent enough or objectively,
certainly flawed.
You say, not consistent enough.
How frequently is information being collected?
Probably, I mean, once a month at best, I'd say we're probably close to like once every two months because of how long it takes them to do it manually, right?
Like they spend like a week, like trying to get, call the people get calls back and then visit a property.
Like, you know what I mean?
It's kind of just
tedious.
No, absolutely.
Is, is the information they're collecting?
Is that how you're pricing your units or are you guys using a pricing tool?
Now, we have a pricing tool.
So we use a IRM through a real page.
The uh and in, in there too, you know, real page has the anonymized uh comp set that only makes up like 10% of the, of what's going into it.
Um, for us, the market survey is more of a, it's more of a supplement to the decisions we're gonna make in a IRM.
Right.
Like knowing kind of what's going on in the market.

Like that system would never see, um, you know, a new property that's offering a special that we could know about or we could see, like in their pricing data and we could choose ourselves to put more weight on what's going on with comps.
Then that how that model would do like, right, I think we could override that then if we wanted to.
Right.
That makes sense.
Um So we actually work with, I would say Justin, you can correct me if I'm wrong.
About 70% of the customers that we work with actually use a, use a pricing tool in conjunction with apartment IQ.
And for the exact reason that you just mentioned, they're essentially using our data to fill the gaps within their pricing tool to help make, make those discussions just more productive.
And if they have questions, in regards to the pricing recommendations, they have our information to back that data.
Um But essentially in regards to the market surveys, we could automate that whole process for you.
It would be updated daily.
So you guys essentially would have a live view to what's going on in your comp sets at any given moment every single time that you logged in.
Um
Justin, did you have any additional questions?
I was gonna say in terms of um uh I think we actually do a really nice job of complementing it.

So you'll see with department IQ I mean, we get very granular and we do show you your specific competitors, but we'll show you, you know, property level data, floor plan level data and then all the way down to the unit level.
So you'll understand some of those anomalies, some of those trends, see, you know, where some of these, the movement the market is coming from.
Um We actually have a report too that a lot of our customers like to have open whenever they're having those weekly or biweekly pricing calls with their representative.
So I'll, I'll touch on that as well.
Um I was gonna ask in terms of your portfolio.
I was trying to do some quick research on it.
Um How many do you guys have a mix of market rate and affordable?
What's kind of the make up there?
Now, we're, now we're, we're all market rate.
Um, we're in kind of like the naturally occurring affordable space but everything is market so cool.

Is that kind of ballpark?
Yeah, it's like 30 I think it's 31 country.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Very cool.
Under 32?
Cool.
Yeah, and, and I was looking back.
So the reason the implementation didn't happen before was because at the time, we didn't have a scraper set up with uh rent manager.
So that's why it looks like the implementation never happened.
Kind of like Jamie said that the products came a long way since then.
Um With roll page though, it's, it's, it's obviously no big deal, but just a reminder with us, um we can track anybody as long as they have a website and use online leasing.
So as long as they have a property management software, um we go out to those websites every single day.
Uh And more specifically, we get the uh the unit level data from the online leasing section of the property website, which is being powered by the property management software.
Yeah, let me take you through it.
There's uh I think the product's gonna look completely different than it did in 2021
but able to see my screen.
Ok.
Yeah.

So, so this is what your apartment IQ landing page would look like each one of your properties actually gets its own individual property card where we run the analytics based off of that property specific comp set.
Now, what's great here is that your teams uh in complete control of your comp set.
So if you want, you can provide us with a list of comps that you've been tracking.
However to your point, maybe you're missing some comps based off of you know, amenities or whatever it might be.
So you can actually uh take advantage of, of our comp matching algorithm.
And essentially what we do is we look at every property in the vicinity of your property and we let you know how well it comps out.
So we, we basically rank every property in the area.
We score from 0 to 100 100 would be the best match with the best comp ever.
A zero would be the worst.
But whenever we come up with these scores, you know, we look at things like location.
So proximity to your own property, we also look at price.
We look at the year the property has been built or renovated.
We also look at asset class and then what else is nice.
And to your point is we also take a look and and and weight the amenities as well.
So a really nice kind of view into into your market.
It's a very analytical approach.
Um I know there's lots of new, new supply coming on the market these days, lots of renovations.
So maybe, you know, a property is a comp it wasn't a Copp or vice versa.
So we can give you some insights into who we think you should be tra uh tracking as well.
But nonetheless, you're in complete control and you always have the ability to add or remove a property from your comp set directly inside the app itself as well.
Um one other question I got for you too, is this, do you think this is something you give access to the on site teams or, or like your regionals or be more so for, for senior leadership?
So uh what I, what I envision is I, I envision Greg and myself sort of driving it and allowing them access to it.

But you know, what I wanna do is I wanna take that sort of subjectivity out of it and, and, and use, you know, like what you said, more of a analytical approach and if there is something from the site teams, like, hey, you know, it's, even though it's not a great comp in here, we are getting a lot of feedback that people are looking at that other site for whatever reason it is, right?

But then we can sort of qualify that against what we're seeing in here and other variables that way there's more of a, of a control and of a boundary, right to where it becomes more of a collective decision on how to look at it.
So that's, that's what I'm envisioning right now.
Yeah, absolutely.
And, and what's nice is since, since the platform is automated, I mean, you can, you can afford to track, you know, one or two or three extra comps.
It doesn't take any more manpower and to your point too, it's nice.
We do offer unlimited users.
Um We don't charge per seat, but you can also grant different permission levels.
So if you want, you can make it.
So an on site team only sees their property or a region only sees their portfolio.
But from there as well, you can actually assign different permission levels where you know, certain users cannot edit comp sets.
So you know, if you guys, you know, you guys want to be in control of comp set, you'd be the ones that would have to actually edit the comp sets.
Whereas the on site teams or the regionals couldn't.
So a little bit of permissions and, and control available for each, for each user.
But like I said, each property gets its own card and then to view the analytics for that property, you click the view button and it'll take you inside the data
once you're in here, then the very top this is gonna be your main navigation.
So each property is four reports associated with it.
Uh The first report which we're on here is the market survey.
And then if you select the data drop down, there's gonna be three additional reports as well.
So this data goes from kind of the high level down to the most granular level.
So property, all property level information, unit type is gonna be all your floor plan level data and the units, it's gonna be all that unit level data.
I'll go over each one high level before I do.
I want you to know that every bit of data in apartment IQ is filterable by these different options.
So you can look at the data based on a specific comp or even a group of comps.
You can also look at it based off of bedrooms, bathrooms, square footage ranges, rent, price ranges, et cetera.
Um Down below all these metrics here are sort from low to high and high to low.
And lastly, we actually give you the ability to toggle on and customize whatever metrics are most important to you.
So I have this set up the way that I like it.
You can see there's a ton of different options here.
But what's nice is that this is user specific.
So, you know, if I have this set up the way that I like it and Frank, you might like it a different way.

That way you can easily make comparisons.
And then all of the data is accessible via vertical scrolling.

The number of units that each property has available right now of those units that are available.
How many are actually vacant down below here?
This is a very big one.
So we'll let you know if there is a rent special or concession attached to the property or attached to a unit in the property.

Um You also see we take it a step further and we'll calculate net effective rent and net effective price per foot for you at the property level, at the floor plan level and all the way down to the unit level.
And lastly, what's nice is we do provide some transactional data in here as well.
So we'll show you things like the number of leases that each property had over the last 30 days, over the last seven days and the number of leases that each property averages per week.
So this would be your higher level property data.
Any questions here?
No.

OK.

basically, since we're tracking each unit individually, if the unit is available on a property website today and it's not tomorrow, we assume that it's a lease.
So unit 123 is there today tomorrow.
Unit 123 is not there.
We count that as a lease
um on a different report we're gonna track or I'm sorry, we're gonna report the least price.
And the way we go about that is we base it off the last lowest advertised price on the property website.
So I'd like to call that out just as a reminder.
We're not actually integrating with your property management software.
We're not getting in there and getting any of that private or shared data.
So this is all based on publicly available information.

Um If that is a concern at all, I do want you to know that we have nothing to do with that.
We're 100% publicly available data.

Like if you have a question where uh a metric or a calculation is coming from, I can pull up the website and show you exactly where we're pulling it from.

So just keep that in mind.

makes sense.

So again, the advantage with us is being able to jump into here and get that reflection of what's going on today rather than yesterday or last week or last month.

One thing too, I'll show you just because uh working with AM but a lot of our AM customers like to use apartment IQ because we could help with pricing their renewals.
So oftentimes I'll hear that uh you know, you price your, your renewals off of historical numbers or off of, you know, present day March 6th numbers.
What you can do here is actually take a look in the future and see how the market's pricing their renewals that are available during the same time frame as your renewals.
So let's pretend you have a couple of two bedrooms that are up for renewal in, in May and you want to see how the market's pricing them, so you can price accordingly.
What you can do here is you can toggle on two bedrooms, you can come on down here to the date available filter.
You could highlight May then all the data will populate based on two bedrooms that are available in May.
So you can see here that the average price per foot is a dollar 49 average, I'm sorry, average rent price is 1473.
What's interesting here is we actually show you the way that our property will be pricing our availability during that same time frame.
So we're actually under price compared to the market.
So it's one of those things that obviously you don't want to be overpriced.
You don't want a car cause a runner to consider looking somebody somewhere else.
But at the same time, you don't wanna underprice them and leave some money on the table.
So just some nice insights from that perspective as well.
Uh I did mention about being able to edit comp sets.

It's, it's a downloadable PDF.

And then inside of here, you know, high level market summary information, high level property insights.

What's the average value of the concession?
